steps.title: Find the original median
steps.content:
With 51 stamps, the median is the 26th value when the ages are ordered. Track the running totals:
  • Age 66: cumulative total 10
  • Age 71: cumulative total 18
  • Age 76: cumulative total 24
  • Age 81: cumulative total 28
The 26th stamp first appears in the age-81 group, so original median = 81.

steps.title: Analyze the median and mean of the new collection
steps.content:
With 52 stamps, the median is the average of the 26th and 27th values.

Both the 26th and 27th stamps remain in the age 81 group (since cumulative frequency reaches 28 at age 81), so new median = 81 — unchanged.

The new 136-year-old stamp is much older than any existing stamp (the oldest in the original collection is at most 96 years).

Adding such a large outlier well above the current distribution pulls the mean upward. New mean > original mean.

steps.title: Identify the answer
steps.content: The mean increased while the median remained at 81. That is the correct answer.
